description: These are the current main headlines from sources across US. Headlines refresh every 900 seconds, so they may lag by a few minutes. For context, this response also includes the two latest AI-generated overviews of the headline landscape, plus yesterday's micro-historical overview.

headlines.headline: A.I. and the Chip Boom: Take a Quiz on New Korean Slang and Memes
headlines.subtitle: South Korea’s chip industry has been supercharged by the advent of the artificial intelligence age, inspiring a host of new phrases.

headlines.headline: OG Anunoby's tip-in with 1.2 seconds left caps greatest comeback in Finals history
U.S. launches new strikes on Iran, hours after Trump issued warning
headlines.subtitle: U.S. Central Command said “multiple targets in Iran” were targeted. Earlier in the day, the president said Tehran had “taken too long to negotiate a deal that would have been great for them.”

headlines.headline: Iran vows to close the Strait of Hormuz to all vessels after US strikes
US launches new strikes on Iran, which fires back at Gulf states and Jordan
headlines.subtitle: The United States launched a second round of airstrikes on Iran and Iran has fired back at Gulf States after President Donald Trump warned that Tehran would “pay the price” for stalled negotiations.

headlines.headline: U.S. says it struck Iranian targets as Tehran claims attacks on American bases
headlines.subtitle: U.S. Central Command maintains the Strait of Hormuz is open despite claims from Iranian navy that the waterway is closed after it attacked two oil tankers.

headlines.headline: Hegseth says U.S. will "negotiate with bombs" if needed; U.S. announces more strikes
headlines.subtitle: The U.S.-Iran ceasefire is appearing to crumble as U.S. forces struck Iran again Wednesday after President Trump pledged Tehran will "pay the price" for not accepting a deal.

headlines.headline: July 1 brings big student loan changes. Here's what you need to know
headlines.subtitle: A popular (and generous) repayment plan ends, two new plans begin and many borrowers will see new loan limits.

headlines.headline: Knicks stage historic NBA Finals comeback to stun Spurs
headlines.subtitle: The New York Knicks took a 3-1 lead in the NBA Finals behind the greatest comeback the NBA championship series has ever seen.

headlines.headline: Trump is accelerating our Social Security insolvency crisis
Knicks pull off historic NBA Finals comeback to put them one win from championship
headlines.subtitle: The Knicks faced a nearly insurmountable lead at halftime.

headlines.headline: The next victim of the Supreme Court’s voting rights decision will be workers
headlines.subtitle: The Trump administration wants to extend Justice Sam Alito’s approach to race to everything.

headlines.subtitle: The State Department is dismantling birth tourism networks across Africa and Europe, officials announced on Wednesday.

Teamsters for a Democratic Union Leaders Explain Their Strategy
headlines.subtitle: As the union reform organization Teamsters for a Democratic Union turns 50, we spoke to its cochairs about their work building rank-and-file power inside one of the most important unions in the United States.

Momentum Builds to Rein In Domestic Spying Law — Whether or Not Bill Pulte Survives as Intel Chief
headlines.subtitle: “I have been doing this a while,” Sen. Ron Wyden told The Intercept. “And I’ve never had this kind of bipartisan support.”

headlines.headline: US Renews Iran Attacks; Tehran Says It Closed Strait of Hormuz
headlines.subtitle:
The United States launched a new wave of attacks against Iran on Wednesday night as efforts to end the three-month old war sputtered, and Tehran hit back by saying it will target any ship going through the Strait of Hormuz.
                [Full Story]

headlines.subtitle: Legacy media are insisting that there’s no evidence of fraud or cheating in California’s recent primary elections. It’s obviously not true.

current.headline: The Strait's Second Night
current.summary: US media overwhelmingly focus on the second night of American airstrikes on Iran, with Iran vowing to close the Strait of Hormuz. Trump threatens more bombing if no deal is reached. Domestic stories include student loan changes and a Knicks playoff game.

previous.headline: The Strait's Covert Fire
previous.summary: US editors are prioritizing President Trump's military confrontation with Iran, including threats of strikes and the revelation of a covert operation (Fox News, Newsmax, CNN, NBC News). Simultaneously, reports highlight inflation hitting 4.2%, a spike Trump defended by linking it to the seizure of Iranian oil (CBS, USA Today, Washington Post, Reason). The Karmelo Anthony murder verdict continues to drive social polarization (The Daily Wire, Newsweek, NY Post).
